titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Spanish Ambassador.

head.hi: The Secretary of State to the Spanish Ambassador.

div.p: My Dear Mr. Ambassador: I beg to acknowledge the receipt of your note of May 10, in which it is stated that your Embassy is in receipt of a telegram from Señsor Armendaiz, under date of May 9, to the effect that the Constitutionalists at Monterey have forced the representative of his firm in that city to give them a draft of $12,000 on Senor Armendaiz, and that, if this draft were not paid, the agent would be put into the penitentiary.

Upon receipt of your note, I am pleased to inform you, a telegram was despatched to the Department’s special representative at El Paso, instructing him to bring this matter to the attention of General
Venustiano Carranza and to use his best efforts to have the latter revoke the order mentioned, thus relieving Senor Armendaiz from the payment of this amount. I am [etc.]
div.closer:
For the Secretary of State:
Robert Lansing .
